data.title: Trump DEA Administrator nominee sidesteps marijuana rescheduling questions
data.description:
Terrance Cole, President Trump's nominee for DEA Administrator, largely sidestepped questions from two Democratic senators regarding marijuana reforms.

Sens. Cory Booker (N.J.) and Alex Padilla (Calif.) asked Cole his position on whether he agrees with a proposal to move cannabis from Schedule I to Schedule III under the Controlled Substances Act (CSA). In a written response, Cole wrote that he would "give the matter careful consideration after consulting with appropriate personnel within the Drug Enforcement Administration, familiarizing myself with the current status of the regulatory process, and reviewing all relevant information."

He was also asked whether he felt the DEA is required to follow the scheduling process as stated in the CSA. "As in all matters, if confirmed, I would look at the individualized facts and circumstances and follow the law and any policies of the Department,” Cole wrote, Marijuana Moment reported.

In a confirmation hearing before the Senate Judiciary Committee in April, Cole noted that he would prioritize an ongoing review at the agency to reclassify marijuana as a less dangerous drug.

Multi-state operators: Ascend Wellness (OTCQX:AAWH), Curaleaf Holdings (OTCPK:CURLF), Cresco Labs (OTCQX:CRLBF), Green Thumb Industries (OTCQX:GTBIF), Trulieve Cannabis (OTCQX:TCNNF), Ayr Wellness (OTCQX:AYRWF), Verano Holdings (OTCQX:VRNOF), and Jushi Holdings (OTCQX:JUSHF).

Cannabis ETFs: AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (NYSEARCA:YOLO), Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (NYSEARCA:MJ), and Amplify Seymour Cannabis ETF (NYSEARCA:CNBS).

data.title: PENN Entertainment faces more criticism from activist investor HG Vora over its strategies
data.description:
Activist investor HG Vora Capital Management issued a letter to shareholders of PENN Entertainment (NASDAQ:PENN) that was sharply critical of the company's leadership and strategy.

As one of Penn’s largest shareholders, HG Vora argued that PEN's (NASDAQ:PENN) stock has significantly underperformed gaming peers over the past decade, attributing the underperformance to failed strategic shifts, poor capital allocation, and mismanagement. 

The letter accuses the current PENN (PENN) board and management, particularly CEO Jay Snowden and Board Chair David Handler, of eroding shareholder value through costly and unsuccessful acquisitions such as Score Media, Barstool Sports, and the ESPN Bet partnership. Despite the investments, PENN's (PENN) online sports betting platform was noted to only hold a 2% market share, while executive compensation has risen dramatically/

HG Vora also alleged that the board has taken steps to entrench itself and avoid accountability, including reducing the number of board seats up for election after HG Vora nominated three independent directors. The letter claims the move is both legally questionable and a breach of fiduciary duty, prompting HG Vora to initiate litigation to restore shareholders' rights.

The activist investor urges shareholders to vote for all three of its independent nominees, arguing that only a reconstituted board can restore accountability and refocus PENN (PENN) on its core strengths in regional casinos and omnichannel gaming.

HG Vora holds a 4.8% stake in PENN.

If HG Vora's campaign succeeds, PENN (PENN) could see a shift in board dynamics, renewed scrutiny of capital allocation and strategy, and potentially a reversal of its digital expansion in favor of its traditional casino operations. The date for the 2025 annual meeting is June 17. BlackRock Advisors is the top shareholder, holding approximately 11.1% of the company's shares. Vanguard Group Inc. follows closely with about 10.7%.

data.title: Evolution Petroleum outlines $850 BOE/day boost from new wells and Tex-Mex acquisition while delaying Chaveroo Block 3 amid volatile oil prices
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Evolution Petroleum Corporation (EPM) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Kelly Loyd, President & CEO, stated that "Our fiscal third quarter results demonstrated Evolution's commitment to disciplined capital allocation and strategic execution. We stayed grounded in our core strengths, allocating capital prudently to high-quality, low-decline assets, maintaining our long-standing dividend and generating positive cash flow." Loyd highlighted the closure of the Tex-Mex acquisition and the addition of four new wells in the second Chaveroo block, noting, "Together, these additions are currently contributing more than 850 net barrels of oil equivalent per day and are expected to meaningfully benefit our fiscal fourth quarter production and cash flow, especially when coupled with the recent strength in natural gas pricing." He emphasized the Tex-Mex transaction's $9 million valuation at "approximately 3.4 times forward adjusted EBITDA" and described the acquisition as "highly accretive... even amid recent oil price volatility."
  • Loyd explained the decision to delay the third Chaveroo development block, saying, "In light of the recent market volatility, we... have made the decision to delay the start of our third development block to later into our fiscal year 2026." He added, "We believe it's prudent to now focus our development activities toward gas-weighted opportunities particularly in the SCOOP/STACK."
  • On dividends, Loyd stated, "On May 12, our Board declared a cash dividend of $0.12 per share of common stock, marking our 47th consecutive quarter of issuing a dividend and our 12th consecutive quarter at $0.12 per share."
  • Mark Bunch, COO, reported, "At SCOOP/STACK, 13 gross wells have come online fiscal year-to-date. We have another five gross wells in progress." He also noted that the four new Chaveroo wells were "drilled and completed on schedule and under budget."
  • Ryan Stash, CFO, said, "In fiscal Q3, we had total revenues of $22.6 million, down 2% year-over-year. The decline was driven primarily by an 8% decrease in production volumes, partially offset by a 7% increase in average realized commodity prices, driven by stronger natural gas and NGL prices." Stash highlighted, "Net loss for the third quarter was $2.2 million or $0.07 per share compared to net income of $0.3 million or $0.01 per share in the year ago period."

Outlook

  • Management indicated that "the fruits of our disciplined acquisition and development strategy during fiscal Q3 will be made obvious in our fiscal fourth quarter when we will see the effects of our Tex-Mex acquisition and our four new Chaveroo wells begin to contribute to our quarterly results."
  • Loyd said the company is "actively pursuing opportunities in what we view as a highly attractive market to acquire oil-weighted load decline producing assets or natural gas properties with favorable hedging potential."
  • The decision to delay Chaveroo Block 3 into fiscal 2026 was described as a response to oil market volatility, with a stated focus on gas-weighted opportunities in the near term.

Financial Results

  • Stash reported, "Adjusted EBITDA was $7.4 million compared to $8.5 million in the year ago period. The decrease was primarily due to lower revenue as a result of lower production volumes and higher total operating costs due to CO2 purchases at Delhi."
  • Cash and cash equivalents totaled $5.6 million and borrowings outstanding under the revolving credit facility were $35.5 million. Total liquidity was $20.1 million at quarter end.
  • The company paid $4.1 million in common stock dividends, made $4 million in repayments on its senior secured credit facility, and incurred $4.4 million in capital expenditures during the quarter.
  • Stash announced, "We have received approval from our lender MidFirst Bank to extend the maturity of our facility to April 2028 and increased their total commitments from $50 million to $55 million. Also, we expect to receive $10 million in additional commitments from a new lender, Prism Bank, which will bring our total commitments to $65 million."

Q&A

  • Jeff Grampp, Northland Capital Markets: Questioned M&A market dynamics and trends separating oil- and gas-weighted acquisitions. Loyd responded that current market conditions favor acquiring low-decline assets, especially with expectations of oil price recovery: "...if oil is going to follow the strip...domestic crude oil production is going to decline...the only way to do that is be the price mechanism, so prices will go higher."
  • Grampp asked about Chaveroo well performance. Bunch replied, "We're about 7 miles away from the 500 wells...these wells have come on really, really good. They're probably about 50% high right now to what we expected them to be producing on average."
  • Chris Degner, Water Tower Research: Asked about the shift to waterflood at Delhi. Loyd answered, "It's on the order of $400,000, $500,000 per month of cost savings to us -- and we don't expect much if any difference in performance."
  • Poe Fratt, Alliance Global Partners: Sought clarification on net production increases and CapEx expectations. Stash indicated it was "a little early for us to put out anything next fiscal year" but reaffirmed the full-year CapEx budget remains valid.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on M&A dynamics, well performance, and cost savings, with no aggressive challenges to management.
  • Management expressed cautious optimism, frequently referencing discipline and flexibility. Loyd stated, "We remain well hedged on oil...providing a strong safety net that supports both our CapEx program and dividend," and also acknowledged volatility: "This volatile market environment underscores the value and resiliency of our diversified portfolio."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts maintained a constructive tone, while management's remarks shifted from enthusiasm for organic growth to a more defensive, disciplined posture amid price volatility and operational delays.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the closure of the Tex-Mex acquisition and the online status of four Chaveroo wells, with immediate impacts flagged for Q4.
  • Guidance shifted with the decision to delay Chaveroo Block 3, representing a pivot in development priorities toward gas-weighted assets.
  • Dividend stability continued, but net loss replaced net income compared to the prior year period.
  • Management’s tone was more cautious, referencing market volatility and the need for disciplined capital allocation, while analysts remained focused on growth and cost management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Oil price volatility and its impact on development timing were highlighted, with management delaying oil-weighted projects to preserve cash flow.
  • Operational risks included weather-related and maintenance downtime, particularly at Barnett and Delhi.
  • Management emphasized diversification and hedging as mitigation strategies, stating a focus on "measured development approach in a low price environment" and cost-control initiatives like the Delhi waterflood transition.

Final Takeaway

Evolution Petroleum’s fiscal third quarter was marked by strategic acquisition and operational execution, positioning the company to benefit from new production in the upcoming quarter. Management’s decision to delay oil-weighted development and focus on gas opportunities reflects a disciplined response to commodity price volatility, while stable dividends and increased liquidity signal a commitment to returning value to shareholders and maintaining financial flexibility in an uncertain market environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Micron expects demand to remain in-line with earlier expectations despite tariffs: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) continues to maintain its previously revealed demand expectations despite tariff concerns and rising competition from China, according to Wells Fargo.

"While MU did not update its F3Q25 guide (we think there has been some expectation of a positive update), we note that MU's overall demand comments were positive, in particular as it relates to Data Center demand trends; industry supply-demand and pricing dynamics," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Aaron Rakers, in an investor note. "In terms of tariff-driven pull-ins, MU only noted that there could be some, but also pointed to the overall demand expectations previously laid out as in line w/ expectations."

Micron presented at J.P. Morgan's Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference on Wednesday morning. The company produces memory and storage solutions for a wide range of products and data centers. Approximately 55% of Micron's revenue during the first half of fiscal year 2025 stemmed from data centers. 

"In terms of China-domiciled competition, which today are focused on legacy DDR4 solutions, Micron noted that it has seen China competitors in the market—this expectation is factored into the company's long-term supply-demand planning," Rakers added. "Micron pointed out that DDR5 solutions are more complex; company focused on the highest value-added/most complex solutions in the market."

Micron is currently working to start up three fabs in the U.S. The locations include Idaho, New York and Virginia. Its fiscal year capital expenditures are expected to total about $14B.

"Micron's Singapore packaging facility broke ground in January and is expected to meaningfully increase advanced packaging capacity in 2027," Rakers noted. "The company has previously noted >50% of y/y capex increase will be driven by construction capex for Idaho and New York fabs, which would not contribute to meaningful bit supply in F2025 and F2026."

Wells Fargo maintains its Overweight rating and $130 price target on Micron.

data.title: Lucid Diagnostics signals expanded market reach and $41M pro forma cash following commercial and clinical advances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lucid Diagnostics (LUCD) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Lishan Aklog stated that Lucid Diagnostics is “better positioned than ever to capitalize on EsoGuard’s really huge clinical and market opportunity,” emphasizing recent progress including securing capital to extend the company’s runway past reimbursement milestones, and the continued expansion of cash-pay and contracted programs with concierge medicine practices and employers. Aklog highlighted, “We performed 3,034 EsoGuard tests in the first quarter. That’s the upper end of our range of 2,500 to 3,000 tests per quarter.” He noted revenue of $800,000, attributing the shortfall to “external headwinds and greater concentration of testing through our healthcare events.”

  • Aklog announced a successful partnership with a major health system to launch a comprehensive EsoGuard esophageal precancer testing program, expanding access to at-risk patients across digestive health teams and a concierge medicine program within the health system. He added, “We doubled our total number of contracts in the past six weeks,” regarding concierge medicine, and “doubled the number of employer contracts in the first quarter.”

  • The CEO described a strengthened balance sheet following a public offering that added approximately $16.1 million in proceeds, resulting in over $40 million in pro forma cash at the end of Q1. Aklog stated, “This importantly extends our runway well into 2026 and past key milestones.”

  • Aklog discussed new clinical data from an NCI-sponsored study showing EsoGuard’s effectiveness in detecting esophageal precancer in at-risk patients without GERD symptoms, stating the study showed “100% negative predictive value, so no false negatives,” and that if replicated, “our already large $60 billion total addressable market could increase by as much as 70%.”

  • CFO Dennis McGrath reported, “cash at the end of the quarter March 31 was $25.2 million,” and noted pro forma cash of about $41 million after the offering. He added, “Our non-GAAP loss for the first quarter of $11.2 million is slightly higher than the trailing four-quarter average of $10.8 million.” McGrath detailed revenue recognition challenges and explained, “With over 3,000 tests for the first quarter we invoiced more than $7.5 million and recognized revenue of approximately $800,000, largely based on the amounts collected during the quarter.”

Outlook

  • Management indicated momentum in engagements with regional commercial insurers on EsoGuard coverage and is awaiting a decision from the MolDx program on Medicare coverage, which Aklog described as “imminent.” He stated, “We’re well positioned to accelerate our commercialization once we secure Medicare coverage.”

  • Aklog forecasted that the impact on revenue from the concierge medicine and employer market contracts would be seen in the second half of the year, emphasizing plans to “expand EsoGuard access on a cash-pay basis.”

Financial Results

  • The company reported 3,034 EsoGuard tests performed and revenue of $800,000 for Q1. McGrath explained, “Collections were lower sequentially and year-over-year, mostly related to delayed cash collections from UnitedHealthcare” and challenges with Kaiser claims. The quarterly burn rate was $11.3 million, and the non-GAAP net loss per share was ($0.16), lower sequentially and compared to the trailing four-quarter average of ($0.20) per share.

  • McGrath reported, “Of the claims submitted in the first quarter, about 60% have been adjudicated, 40% are pending…about 30% resulted in an allowable amount by the insurance company with an average of about $1,362 per test.”

Q&A

  • Mark Massaro, BTIG: Questioned Q1 volumes, weather impacts, and volume trajectory for Q2. Aklog responded there was “no evidence…that weather was a factor,” and said, “I would still recommend that we sort of assume that we’ll be in this range until either we get Medicare...or as the commercial coverage, as we start to get broadening commercial coverage, at which point we’ll look to drive volume beyond these levels.”

  • Massaro, BTIG: Asked about Palmetto GBA’s Medicare decision timeline. Aklog clarified, “We haven’t gone back and engaged with the MolDx group. It wasn’t a meeting. We had multiple meetings and multiple engagements heading up to the submission…we still think it’s coming soon.”

  • Kyle Mikson, Canaccord: Inquired about ASP trends and mix. McGrath explained, “the contribution to the current revenue numbers in the ASP is minimal for this quarter for the first quarter.”

  • Mikson, Canaccord: Asked about Medicare claim mix. McGrath said, “percentage of Medicare is lower. It’s probably right now, 10% – 12% to 15% of our volume, but obviously the goal there is 40%.”

  • Mike Matson, Needham: Asked about contingency plans if Medicare approval is delayed. Aklog replied, “we are building this parallel pipeline of concierge and employer contracted revenue to drive revenue…we’re well positioned to handle any delays.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to slightly positive tone, exploring volume sustainability, reimbursement timelines, and commercial channel development, with persistent focus on execution milestones and cautious optimism regarding Medicare coverage.

  • Management's tone was consistently confident during prepared remarks, with Aklog using phrases such as “we really believe we’re set up very well” and “we feel really confident and like we are really well-positioned.” During Q&A, the tone remained optimistic but cautious and at times explanatory, clarifying process details and timelines.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management continued to express confidence, though the language shifted to more immediate anticipation of Medicare coverage and greater emphasis on new channel expansion. Analyst tone remained inquisitive, focused on execution and reimbursement timing.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 saw lower revenue ($800,000) and test volume (3,034) compared to Q4’s $1.2 million and over 4,000 tests. Management continued to highlight the need to maintain test volumes at a level sufficient for payer engagement, with a greater strategic push into concierge and employer channels.

  • Strategic focus evolved from initial contract wins and pipeline development in Q4 to tangible increases in contracts and partnerships in Q1. Confidence in securing Medicare coverage heightened, with management now describing it as “imminent.”

  • Analysts' focus shifted from questions about scaling post-Medicare approval in Q4 to more granular inquiries about contract execution, ASP dynamics, and reimbursement challenges in Q1.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited delayed cash collections from major insurers like UnitedHealthcare and ongoing claim denials with Kaiser as impacting collections and revenue recognition.

  • The company’s revenue remains sensitive to reimbursement processes, with McGrath reiterating, “Key determinant in how revenue is recognized at this point in our reimbursement journey is the probability of collection.”

  • Aklog noted the continued importance of Medicare coverage, stating, “the most important mitigating factor is that we raised capital to get us through these milestones.”

Final Takeaway

Lucid Diagnostics’ management emphasized strong positioning for commercial expansion, citing a strengthened balance sheet with over $40 million in pro forma cash and the expectation of imminent Medicare coverage for EsoGuard. With tangible growth in concierge and employer contracts and new clinical data potentially expanding the addressable market, the company is focused on accelerating revenue through diversified channels while maintaining steady test volumes and preparing for significant reimbursement milestones in the months ahead.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Snowflake, MongoDB see better cloud engagement growth in April, Microsoft 365 growth slows - BofA
data.description:
BofA said the April data showed resilient cloud growth, consistent with results from Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and other cloud infrastructure vendors in the first quarter of calendar year 2025.

BofA's cloud consumption group includes companies Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG), MongoDB Atlas (NASDAQ:MDB) and Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW). Meanwhile, the firm's Desktop group consists of Adobe Creative Cloud and Firefly (NASDAQ:ADBE), Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK), Intuit QuickBooks and Mailchimp (NASDAQ:INTU) and Microsoft 365 (NASDAQ:MSFT).

Analysts led by Brad Sills said that in the cloud consumption group, April data suggests that average consumption held in from March, with engaged visit growth of +8.5% year-over-year in April versus +9.3% year-over-year in March. However, the month-over-month growth of +0.4% in April was stronger than the normal seasonality of -6.4%.

The analysts added that they are not expecting a material macro-driven consumption slowdown, given that the industry is still emerging from a two-and-a-half-year period of cloud optimization.

Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) engaged visits grew -4.9% year-over-year, decelerating from -5.2% in March, with -7.2% month-over-month below -5.4% seasonality. The data is somewhat contradictory to the company's management commentary that April usage growth was consistent with year-to-date tends, the analysts noted.

For MongoDB Atlas (NASDAQ:MDB), end-user engaged visits growth of +6.1% year-over-year in April largely held from a healthy +6.3% in March. The Atlas metric points to sustained recovery since usage headwinds were seen in the first quarter of 2024, according to the analysts.

Snowflake's (NYSE:SNOW) total page view growth of +24.3% year-over-year accelerated meaningfully from 16.4%. The month-over-month growth of +5.5% is well ahead of monthly seasonality of -12.7%. The April results point to a sustained recovery in consumption since the January quarter, the analysts added.

Sills and his team said that in the desktop group, April engaged visit growth of +4.9% year-over-year declined sharply from +12.4%  year-over-year in March. In addition, the month-over-month growth of -10.1% was weaker than the normal April seasonality of -5%. The result, perhaps, validates that small and medium-sized businesses, or SMB, demand has slowed a bit, consistent with HubSpot's first quarter results.

The analysts added that Microsoft 365 (MSFT) engaged visit growth in April of +31.1% year-over-year is down from a strong +43.5% year-over-year in March 2025. However, April growth of -5.4% month-over-month came in slightly better than April seasonality of -5.8%. April represented the third consecutive month of better seasonal growth for Microsoft 365.

Engaged visits for Autodesk increased 18.1% year-over-year in April to 9.2 million but declined -5.6% month-over-month, the analysts noted

Engaged visits for Adobe Firefly of 6.2 million (+30.8% year-over-year) was down from 6.8 million in March. However, April represents the third consecutive month with engaged visits of more than 6 million (versus the prior 4 million to 5 million range), suggesting heightened levels of engagement. The combined Creative Cloud and Firefly suggests net year-over-year growth of -19% in April declined from -11.6% in March, the analysts added.

data.title: Merck wins FDA label expansion for Welireg in neuroendocrine tumors
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on Wednesday approved Merck’s (NYSE:MRK) anti-cancer agent Welireg (belzutifan) as a treatment for rare neuroendocrine tumors, pheochromocytoma, or paraganglioma.

Accordingly, the oral therapy will be indicated for those aged 12 and older with locally advanced, unresectable, or metastatic pheochromocytoma and paraganglioma (PPGL) in the U.S.

The decision comes after the FDA granted its priority review for Merck’s (NYSE:MRK) supplemental New Drug Application targeting the U.S. approval of Welireg for the above conditions.

The sNDA was backed by data from the company’s Phase 2 LITESPARK-015 trial, which indicated an objective response rate of 26% for Welireg monotherapy among 72 patients, including those with advanced PPGL.

The approval comes ahead of the May 26 target action date the FDA had set for the review. Welireg was previously approved in the U.S. for the rare genetic condition, von Hippel-Lindau disease, and a form of kidney cancer called advanced renal cell carcinoma.

data.title: ICMB signals more stable earnings profile and targets NII growth through platform expansion in H2 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Investcorp Credit Management BDC, Inc. (ICMB) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Suhail Shaikh reported the quarter "marked a period of continued progress and NAV stability for ICMB," with priorities centered around resolving legacy credit issues and repositioning the portfolio for steady performance. Shaikh stated "Our net asset value per share increased $0.03 per share to $5.42 compared to $5.39 as of December 31, 2024." He attributed the NAV increase to "an increased nonrealized gain, offset by a decline in net investment income per share, which was largely due to reduced investment activity during the quarter and a continuation of repayments."
  • Shaikh highlighted a significant improvement in portfolio credit quality: "We have significantly reduced the number of nonaccruals and now have just 2 investments in nonaccrual status, representing approximately 1.7% of the total portfolio at fair value, down from 5 investments and 3.6% of fair value in the previous quarter."
  • The CEO emphasized a "noticeable slowdown in new deal activity" due to tariff concerns and geopolitical uncertainty, but underlined a disciplined, selective investment approach. Shaikh commented, "We believe patience and discipline in this environment will ultimately be rewarded."
  • Shaikh provided an update on tariff exposure, stating "less than 20% of our portfolio may experience moderate direct effects from tariffs," and noted portfolio companies are actively implementing mitigation strategies.
  • COO Andrew Muns detailed portfolio activity, including investment in 1 new and 2 existing portfolio companies, with fundings totaling $5.1 million at cost and a weighted average yield of approximately 10.2%. Muns reported full realization of 3 investments with $7.3 million in proceeds and an IRR of approximately 9.6%.
  • CFO Walter Tsin reported "the fair value of our portfolio was $192.4 million compared to $191.6 million on December 31." Tsin added, "Our net assets were $78.1 million, an increase of $0.5 million from the prior quarter. Our portfolio's net increase in net assets from operations this quarter was approximately $2.2 million."
  • Tsin announced, "on April 15, 2025, the Board of Directors declared a distribution for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, of $0.12 per share, payable in cash on June 14, 2025, to all stockholders of record as of May 24, 2025."

Outlook

  • Shaikh stated, "we expect more stable earnings profile for the remainder of 2025, notwithstanding any macro shocks."
  • The CEO projected the second half of the year will bring "interesting investment opportunities as volatility in the market abates."
  • Shaikh confirmed efforts to raise another pool of capital are "live and underway," targeting platform expansion and overhead absorption as a "second half 2025 event."

Financial Results

  • Net investment income before taxes was $0.7 million or $0.05 per share, compared to $0.06 per share in the prior quarter.
  • The weighted average of the debt portfolio was 10.8%, up from 10.4% in the previous quarter.
  • Gross leverage was 1.53x and net leverage was 1.37x as of March 31, compared to 1.57x gross and 1.42x net in the previous quarter.
  • The company reported $13 million in cash as of March 31, with $10.7 million restricted and $44 million available under the revolving credit facility.
  • Portfolio consisted of 43 borrowers, with 77% in first lien debt and 23% in equity warrants and other positions.

Q&A

  • Paul Johnson, KBW: "What sort of things could you talk about maybe you're waiving a little bit of fees here, but is there room from the adviser to raise additional fees?..." Suhail Shaikh: "That's always something that we can consider...we continue to work on scaling the private credit platform at Investcorp...there will be a natural absorption of overhead and expenses...We've done the stability...now we're very focused on...increasing NII every which way possible."
  • Paul Johnson, KBW: "Do you have any kind of idea maybe into the runway for that...shareholders might start to see some benefit from that, if you think that's a 2025 sort of event or it's going to be a longer process?" Suhail Shaikh: "It’s a 2025 event. We are currently in the process of raising another pool of capital...So it is a second half 2025 event."
  • Christopher Nolan, Ladenburg Thalmann: "When you said you're raising capital, are you talking about equity or debt?" Suhail Shaikh: "Not for this vehicle, Christopher. We're raising capital for other vehicles, which will help us sort of expand the platform and have some overhead absorption. So that's equity capital that we're raising for..."
  • Christopher Nolan, Ladenburg Thalmann: "Why don't you just do share repurchases at this point given the dividend yield and everything else?" Suhail Shaikh: "That's something we talk about. We won't take it off the table. There are no plans in place yet, but it's obviously a tool that we have and something we consider all the time."
  • Christopher Nolan: "The nonaccrual exits, congratulations on that. Should we see a bump up in NII in the second quarter." Suhail Shaikh: "You should. I mean...we’re starting to see about 25, 50 basis points of widening on new opportunities. So you might see a little bit of an increase in NII..."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised pressing questions about expense structure, fee waivers, capital raising, and potential share repurchases, with a tone that was mostly neutral but occasionally pressing for specifics, particularly on the timing and impact of strategic initiatives and expense management.
  • Management maintained a measured and confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing "NAV stability" and a "more stable earnings profile," but expressed cautious optimism in Q&A, noting "as volatility in the market abates," and "it’s a 2025 event."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management remained focused on sustainability of earnings, but the current quarter saw more emphasis on the timeline for platform expansion and direct questions about capital actions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from "capital preservation and NAV stability" in Q2 to "maintaining NAV stability, sustainable net investment income while selectively deploying capital" in Q3, with explicit focus on a stable earnings profile and reduced nonaccruals.
  • Strategic focus evolved to emphasize overhead absorption and platform expansion as levers for future NII growth, with management setting a "second half 2025" expectation.
  • Key metrics showed improvement: nonaccruals reduced from 5 investments to 2, fair value of portfolio and net assets increased modestly, and leverage ratios improved slightly.
  • Analyst focus shifted from dividend sustainability and yield trends to more granular questions about expense allocation and share repurchase plans.
  • Management's confidence increased as credit issues were resolved, while analysts pressed more for tactical timeframes and actions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Shaikh identified "heightened uncertainty, reduced M&A and financing activity" as key market risks impacting new deal flow.
  • Less than 20% of the portfolio may experience moderate direct effects from tariffs, with mitigation strategies actively being implemented by affected companies.
  • Management cited macro shocks as a potential risk to earnings stability and continues to monitor exposure through "engaging with portfolio companies to monitor and manage these risks going forward."
  • Analysts raised concerns about expense absorption, fee waivers, and the sustainability of NII in light of muted investment activity.

Final Takeaway

Investcorp Credit Management BDC, Inc. enters the second half of 2025 positioned for stability, having reduced nonaccruals and improved portfolio quality while maintaining a cautious but optimistic stance amid market uncertainty. Management signaled that ongoing platform expansion and overhead absorption are expected to drive net investment income growth as the year progresses, while ongoing risk management and selective investment remain key priorities to navigate macroeconomic headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Waldencast reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Waldencast press release (NASDAQ:WALD): Q1 net Loss was $20.7M primarily driven by depreciation and financial charges. 
  • Revenue of $65.4M (-4.2% Y/Y).

data.title: AFCG outlines $287M active pipeline while maintaining selective originations amid sector volatility
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Advanced Flower Capital Inc. (AFCG) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Robyn Tannenbaum, President and CIO, reported that "we continue to see many capital markets participants remaining on the sidelines as the lack of clarity around federal reform continues," driving a "growing supply and demand imbalance for capital across the sector with demand outpacing an already limited supply." She added that "as of May 1, 2025, our active pipeline has $287 million of deals" and reiterated the company’s focus on backing operators with a track record of success and selectively providing construction financing.
  • Daniel Neville, CEO, stated that AFCG generated distributable earnings of $0.21 per basic weighted average share for Q1 2025 and highlighted the continued impact from underperforming legacy loans. He noted a recent $14 million senior secured credit facility commitment to Standard Wellness Holdings, supporting acquisitions and refinancing activities, reflecting ongoing efforts to diversify the portfolio.
  • Neville further detailed that the company continues to manage the liquidation process for private company A and has seen progress on asset sales, while with private company K, the receiver is in discussions for asset sales. He also described ongoing legal proceedings with Justice Grown, stating, "we are currently evaluating the best course of action to protect our shareholders' capital under the credit facility and have nothing further to share on the pending litigation at this time."
  • Brandon Hetzel, CFO, reported, "for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, we generated net interest income of $6.6 million and distributable earnings of $4.5 million or $0.21 per basic weighted average common share, and had a GAAP net income of $4.1 million or $0.18 per basic weighted average common share." Hetzel disclosed a portfolio of $372.5 million principal outstanding across 18 loans as of May 1, 2025, with a weighted average portfolio yield of approximately 18%.

Outlook

  • Management indicated that originations may be muted due to market volatility and a limited supply of capital. Tannenbaum emphasized, "we are focused on sourcing deals and backing operators with a prior track record of success and selectively providing construction financing."
  • Neville explained that originations will be approached opportunistically, stating, "originating simply for the sake of originating to hit a target is probably not the right idea given the market volatility."
  • There were no explicit forward-looking financial metrics or guidance figures for EPS or revenue disclosed for upcoming quarters.

Financial Results

  • AFCG generated net interest income of $6.6 million and distributable earnings of $4.5 million for Q1 2025. GAAP net income was $4.1 million. The company ended Q1 with $366.3 million in principal outstanding across 17 loans; as of May 1, that figure was $372.5 million across 18 loans.
  • The weighted average portfolio yield to maturity remained at approximately 18%. The CECL reserve was $29.9 million or about 9.75% of loans at carrying value, with total unrealized loss on the balance sheet of $20.4 million for loans held at fair value.
  • The company renewed its senior secured credit facility, which can expand to $100 million, maturing April 2028 and bearing interest at prime plus 50 basis points, with a prime floor of 6.5%.
  • Total assets were $321.7 million and book value per share was $8.89 at quarter end.

Q&A

  • Pablo Zuanic, Zuanic & Associates, asked about the decline in interest income from $9.2 million in Q4 to $8.5 million in Q1, and whether there were non-recurring fees. Hetzel explained, "in the fourth quarter of 2024, we did receive more income related to subsidiary of private company G... In Q4, it was approximately $1.6 million. In Q1, it was about $600,000. The other driver is, in Q4, there were dividends up from the TRS, which we did not have in the first quarter."
  • Zuanic inquired about distributable earnings per share relative to the dividend. Neville stated, "we're going to be very opportunistic in our originations and we'll be looking really to find and source high-quality borrowers and lend to proven operators... we think that originating simply for the sake of originating to hit a target is probably not the right idea."
  • Zuanic also asked about expected inflows from the sale of assets related to private company A and K. Tannenbaum and Neville declined to provide specifics, citing receiver control and court processes.
  • Zuanic probed ongoing litigation with Justice Grown. Katz responded, "it's early in this pending litigation, and we're not prepared to provide any comment or description beyond what we've included" in public filings.
  • Chris Muller, Citizens Capital Markets, questioned the opportunity for private lenders if Pennsylvania adopts a state-run model; Neville responded, "the Senate has been very steadfast and unsupportive of a state-run model. And so, I don't view that as the likely outcome... and therefore, not an opportunity for us."
  • Muller asked about pipeline growth and deal selectivity; Tannenbaum noted that pipeline figures are cumulative, all cannabis deals, and that origination targets are not being set due to market volatility.
  • On tariffs, Neville explained that the impact is "pretty de minimis" for the company’s borrowers since key input costs are largely sourced stateside.
  • An unidentified analyst for Aaron Grey, Alliance Global Partners, asked about timing and expected yields for new originations. Tannenbaum said no timing guidance would be provided, while Neville described yields as generally consistent with recent originations but highlighted a focus on quality operators.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts adopted a neutral to slightly negative tone, pressing for clarity on income, originations, and problem loan recoveries while receiving limited guidance or specifics from management.
  • Management maintained a cautious and controlled tone, often deflecting requests for specifics on timing and proceeds from asset sales, and repeatedly citing external factors and ongoing processes. Phrases such as "we're not going to give any guidance around timing" and "we are not prepared to provide any comment" indicated defensiveness and a focus on risk management.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained consistently cautious, with a continued emphasis on selectivity and risk mitigation. Analysts remained persistent in seeking granularity on asset recovery and earnings trajectory.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The active pipeline decreased from over $380 million in Q4 2024 to $287 million in Q1 2025, reflecting muted origination activity amid ongoing sector volatility.
  • Distributable earnings per share fell from $0.29 to $0.21, and net interest income from $7.6 million to $6.6 million, attributed mainly to lower income from specific borrowers and the absence of certain non-recurring items.
  • Portfolio size increased slightly in terms of loans outstanding, while the weighted average yield remained stable at 18%.
  • Management’s messaging shifted from a focus on active origination and portfolio diversification in Q4 to a more defensive stance, emphasizing principal protection and selectivity in Q1.
  • Analysts’ questions continued to focus on asset recovery, earnings sustainability, and origination outlook, reflecting ongoing concerns about portfolio risk and revenue visibility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted continued underperformance of legacy loans and the impact on distributable earnings.
  • Ongoing litigation with Justice Grown presents uncertainty regarding the recovery of capital from one of the company’s significant loans.
  • Liquidation processes for private companies A and K are subject to court and receiver decisions, limiting management’s ability to provide guidance on timing or amounts of capital recovery.
  • Scarcity of capital in the cannabis sector and muted originations due to market volatility remain central challenges.

Final Takeaway

Advanced Flower Capital’s Q1 2025 call underscored a strategic shift toward heightened selectivity and risk management amid persistent sector volatility and a constrained capital environment. Management highlighted progress in portfolio diversification and the pursuit of high-quality borrowers, but distributable earnings and interest income declined quarter-over-quarter, reflecting the ongoing impact of underperforming loans and non-recurring income. With an active pipeline of $287 million and a consistent focus on protecting shareholder capital, the company remains cautious on new originations and asset recovery timelines, prioritizing prudent deployment over growth targets in the near term.
